chunk_summary: This section focuses on diagnostic decision rules and resource-adapted test interpretation for inpatient management of severe acute malnutrition (SAM), emphasizing TB and HIV evaluation when feeding and antibiotics fail, per national guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand diagnostic decision rules for severe acute malnutrition (SAM) in inpatient settings.
Learn resource-adapted test interpretation for TB and HIV in malnourished children.
Recognize the limitations of TST in severe malnutrition and alternative diagnostic approaches.
Apply national algorithms for targeted evaluation in cases of non-response to feeding and antibiotics.
key_facts: Failure to respond to feeding and antibiotics should prompt TB and HIV evaluation per national algorithms.
Tuberculin skin test (TST) can yield false negatives in cases of severe malnutrition.
National Tuberculosis Elimination Programme provides specific guidelines for pediatric TB management.
Resource-adapted diagnostic approaches are critical in low-resource settings for SAM management.
Operational guidelines for SAM management were established by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare in 2011.
reference_quote: Failure to respond to verified feeding and antibiotics should trigger targeted evaluation for TB and HIV per national algorithms, recognizing that TST can be falsely negative in severe malnutrition.
chunk_heading: SEVERE ACUTE MALNUTRITION-(SAM) MANAGEMENT;INPATIENT STABILIZATION AND EARLY MONITORING;DIAGNOSTIC DECISION RULES AND RESOURCE-ADAPTED TEST  INTERPRETATION;
